classifications.note: The agent parsed a write response through .items[0].uuid instead of the documented data envelope, leaving dependent heading/to-do commands without the created project UUID.
The agent treated the to-do's tags array as its effective tags and did not union tags inherited from its project and area.
The agent inferred Today membership from todaySection even though the item had active disposition and no explicit schedule; todaySection alone is not view membership.
The write succeeded only after failed attempts to resolve a relative weekday using unavailable Python and BSD-specific date syntax.

rationale: This adds only the missing interpretation and construction rules exposed by the failures: the JSON envelope path, summary-versus-detail tags, authoritative view fields, structured replies, explicit area movement, and an outside-in nested-placement pattern. It avoids benchmark-specific titles and preserves existing behavior.

classifications.note: The agent discovered the relevant verbs but lacked a compact construction pattern for nested project, heading, and to-do placement.
The agent verified completion but did not apply the model rule that completed items belong in Logbook, likely distracted by logged:false.
The agent read the Inbox but did not reliably extract the matching row from the JSON envelope.
The agent treated tags in a search row as the complete effective tag set instead of inspecting full detail and inheritance.
The agent misread todaySection as view membership; an active undated item belongs to Anytime.
The agent first addressed the read envelope as .items instead of .data and then had to discover move placement syntax.
The agent understood --when but incurred errors while resolving a relative weekday into the required ISO date.

rationale: These edits make three existing contracts operationally explicit: grouped Today output, authoritative effective-tag/view evidence, and safe construction and recovery for nested writes. A single portability note removes the observed relative-date error without prescribing workflow.

classifications.note: A malformed child step was followed by replaying already-successful parent creation, producing duplicate projects and headings instead of retrying only the failed command.
The agent found the correct command but did not account for Today JSON being grouped under data.today and data.evening.
The agent treated summary-row tags as effective tags instead of inspecting full detail for inherited tags.
The agent interpreted todaySection as view membership despite the undated start: active record being Anytime.
Relative-date construction used unavailable Python and then nonportable date arithmetic before succeeding.

rationale: These edits make three hidden structural rules explicit: Today output is grouped, summary fields cannot establish effective tags or views, and create commands use one positional title plus parent flags. The recovery sentence prevents duplicate state when a compound write partially succeeds, while the runtime note removes a recurring relative-date construction error.

classifications.note: Parent references were supplied as positional arguments, and successful prefix creates were repeated after downstream failures, producing duplicates.
The grouped Today JSON shape was not converted into the requested combined title array.
The agent treated summary-row tags as the complete effective tag set instead of inspecting full detail.
The agent interpreted todaySection as view membership despite start active with no startDate indicating Anytime.
Relative-date construction used unavailable or incompatible runtime utilities before succeeding.

rationale: The dominant cross-task failure is misreading the universal JSON envelope, especially using .items or .item and then constructing empty references. Naming the exact result path in the top-level help fixes that root cause at the first surface every agent inspected, without changing command behavior.

classifications.note: The agent extracted IDs from nonexistent .items/.item fields, so subsequent write arguments were empty.
The agent queried .items instead of the JSON envelope's .data array.
The matching item was returned, but the agent did not reliably interpret the structured result and overdue deadline.
The agent treated search-result direct tags as the complete effective tag set instead of inspecting detail for inherited tags.
The agent did not translate absent container fields and start=active from the JSON detail into none/anytime/actionable.
The agent assumed Inbox required a destination option and invented unsupported flags before recovering.

rationale: Two failed tasks share the same root cause: agents correctly selected commands but guessed a conventional .items/.item JSON shape instead of the documented envelope's .data payload. Putting the shape directly on the top-level --json signpost is the smallest general fix and also clarifies how to interpret omitted optional fields.

classifications.note: The agent assumed read and mutation envelopes used .items/.item, producing empty references and aborting the compound write.
The correct Inbox command was used, but the agent queried .items instead of the envelope payload at .data.
The agent found the relevant Today data but did not use or reproduce the documented overdue semantics.
The agent treated directly attached tags as the effective tag set and missed inherited container tags.
The agent did not interpret absent container fields as no container or start=active as Anytime/actionable.
The agent did not understand that todo add without a destination creates an Inbox to-do.

rationale: A single conventions edit clarifies the misunderstood data-model boundaries: headings are project-scoped, Inbox is a view, effective tags require container inheritance, and todaySection is not list membership. It changes no execution behavior or write safety.

classifications.note: The agent treated an area as a project and did not understand that headings are project-scoped.
The agent treated Inbox as an area rather than a built-in view.
The agent reported direct tags without combining tags inherited from the containing project and area.
The agent incorrectly interpreted todaySection as Today membership despite start=active indicating Anytime for an unscheduled open item.

rationale: One existing data-semantics convention is made operational and explicit. It addresses the recurring confusion between built-in views and containers, direct and inherited tags, and todaySection versus actual view membership without adding workflow advice or changing behavior.

classifications.note: The agent treated an area as a project, attempted to target a to-do by title where a UUID was required, and did not create the project before its headings.
The agent treated Inbox as an area instead of a built-in view, causing an avoidable failed search before recovering with read_view.
The agent reported only the item's direct tags and did not combine tags inherited from its project and area.
The agent interpreted todaySection as view membership despite the item being open, active, and unscheduled.
